column1: Comparing three memory cap policy approaches

column2: Memory keeps increasing for each new container following the exponential pattern
column7: Most realistic approach, but it eventually causes starvation

column2: Memory growth restarts after every 4 containers
column7: Prevents starvation, but it is less realistic for real workloads

column2: Memory grows until it reaches a 2048 MB limit per container
column7: Best balance between realism and fairness, so this is the recommended approach
